auth0client.title: Auth0Client
auth0client.description: The main server-side Auth0 client. Create one instance and share it across your entire app. The recommended pattern is to create the instance in `lib/auth0.ts` and import it wherever needed: Server Components, Server Actions, Route Handlers, middleware, and Pages Router API routes. **Setup using environment variables (recommended):** Set these in `.env.local`: ``` AUTH0_DOMAIN=your-tenant.us.auth0.com AUTH0_CLIENT_ID=your_client_id AUTH0_CLIENT_SECRET=your_client_secret AUTH0_SECRET=a_32_byte_hex_secret APP_BASE_URL=http://localhost:3000 ``` Then create the client with no arguments: ```ts // lib/auth0.ts import { Auth0Client } from '@auth0/nextjs-auth0/server'; export const auth0 = new Auth0Client(); ``` **Setup passing options directly:** ```ts export const auth0 = new Auth0Client({ domain: 'your-tenant.us.auth0.com', clientId: 'YOUR_CLIENT_ID', clientSecret: 'YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET', secret: 'YOUR_32_BYTE_SECRET', appBaseUrl: 'http://localhost:3000', }); ``` **Built-in routes (served by `middleware()`):** Once you mount `auth0.middleware(req)` in `middleware.ts`, the following routes are handled automatically: | Route | Description | |---|---| | `GET /auth/login` | Redirects the user to Auth0 to log in. Add `?returnTo=/path` to control the post-login redirect. | | `GET /auth/logout` | Logs the user out and clears the session. | | `GET /auth/callback` | Handles the OAuth callback from Auth0 after login. | | `GET /auth/profile` | Returns the logged-in user's profile as JSON. | | `GET /auth/access-token` | Returns a fresh access token for the current session as JSON. | | `POST /auth/backchannel-logout` | Handles Auth0 backchannel logout notifications. | To trigger login from a link or button: ```tsx Log in Log in and go to dashboard Log out ``` Route paths can be customized via the `routes` option in `Auth0ClientOptions`.

middleware.title: middleware
middleware.description:
Handles all Auth0 routes (login, logout, callback, profile, access-token) from Next.js middleware.
Call this from `middleware.ts` at the root of your project so every auth route is served automatically.

parameters.name: req
parameters.description: The incoming Next.js request from middleware.

returns.description: A `NextResponse` handled by the matching auth route, or a passthrough response for all other routes.

throws.description: if a `DomainResolver` function throws or returns an invalid value. Only applies when using Multiple Custom Domains.
if the resolved domain is an IP address, localhost, or contains a path/port. Only applies when using Multiple Custom Domains.

get-session.title: getSession
get-session.description:
Returns the active session, or `null` if the user is not logged in.

**App Router** (Server Components, Server Actions, Route Handlers): call with no arguments.
The SDK reads the session cookie via Next.js `cookies()` automatically.

**Pages Router / middleware**: pass `req` so the SDK can read cookies from the request.

The session contains:
- `user`: the logged-in user's profile (`sub`, `name`, `email`, `picture`, etc.)
- `tokenSet`: the current access token, its expiry, and scope

returns.description: The session object, or `null` if no active session exists.

throws.description: if the session was created on a different domain. Treat this the same as a missing session. Only applies when using Multiple Custom Domains.
if the ID token issuer does not match the resolved domain. Only applies when using Multiple Custom Domains.

get-access-token.title: getAccessToken
get-access-token.description:
Returns the access token for the current user, refreshing it automatically if expired.

**App Router** (Server Components, Server Actions, Route Handlers): call with no `req`/`res`.
The SDK reads cookies automatically. Note: if a token refresh happens inside a Server Component,
the updated token cannot be persisted because Server Components cannot set cookies. Use middleware
with `getAccessToken(req, res)` when persistence after refresh is required.

**Pages Router / middleware**: pass both `req` and `res` so the refreshed token can be
written back to the session cookie.

throws.description: if the user is not authenticated.
if the token is expired and there is no refresh token.
if Auth0 rejects the refresh attempt.

if Auth0 requires MFA step-up before issuing the token. Catch this and use `err.mfa_token` to drive the MFA flow via `auth0.mfa`. The `err.mfa_requirements` property lists available challenge types and enrollment options.

get-access-token-for-connection.title: getAccessTokenForConnection
get-access-token-for-connection.description:
Returns an access token scoped to a specific social connection (e.g. Google, GitHub).
Use this when you need to call a third-party provider API on behalf of the logged-in user.

**App Router**: call with `options` only. The SDK reads cookies automatically.

**Pages Router / middleware**: pass `req` and `res` as additional arguments so the refreshed
token can be written back to the session cookie.

**Prerequisite:** The connection must be enabled on your Auth0 application and the user must
have authorized it during login.

throws.description: if the user is not authenticated.
if no refresh token is available to exchange.
if Auth0 rejects the token exchange.

update-session.title: updateSession
update-session.description:
Overwrites the current user's session. Throws if no session exists.

**App Router** (Server Actions, Route Handlers): pass the updated `session` object only.
The SDK writes the new session cookie automatically.

**Pages Router / middleware**: pass `req`, `res`, and the updated `session` so the SDK
can write the cookie to the response.
update-session.signature: updateSession(req: NextRequest | Request | PagesRouterRequest, res: Response | NextResponse<unknown> | PagesRouterResponse, session: SessionData): Promise<void>

parameters.name: req
parameters.description: Pages Router or middleware request. Omit in App Router.

parameters.name: res
parameters.description: Pages Router or middleware response. Omit in App Router.

parameters.name: session
parameters.description: The updated session data. Include the full session shape (spread existing and override fields).

throws.description: if the user is not authenticated.

custom-token-exchange.title: customTokenExchange
custom-token-exchange.description:
Exchanges an external token for Auth0 tokens using Custom Token Exchange (RFC 8693).

This is a server-only method. It does not create or modify the user's session; the returned
tokens are for the caller to use directly.

returns.description: The exchange response with `accessToken` and optionally `idToken` and `refreshToken`.

throws.description: if `subjectToken` is empty.
if `subjectTokenType` is not a valid URI.
if `actorToken` is provided without `actorTokenType`.
if Auth0 rejects the exchange.

mfa.title: mfa
mfa.description:
Server-side MFA client. Access this when `getAccessToken()` throws a `MfaRequiredError`
and you need to drive the MFA challenge-response flow from the server.

Methods:
- `getAuthenticators({ mfaToken })`: list the user's enrolled MFA factors. Throws `MfaGetAuthenticatorsError` on failure.
- `challenge({ mfaToken, challengeType, authenticatorId? })`: initiate a challenge. Throws `MfaChallengeError` on failure.
- `verify({ mfaToken, otp? | oobCode+bindingCode? | recoveryCode? })`: complete the challenge. Throws `MfaVerifyError` (code `"invalid_grant"`) on wrong code.
- `enroll({ mfaToken, authenticatorTypes, ... })`: enroll a new factor. Throws `MfaEnrollmentError` on failure.

All `auth0.mfa` methods throw `MfaTokenExpiredError` (code `"mfa_token_expired"`) if the MFA token has expired,
and `MfaTokenInvalidError` (code `"mfa_token_invalid"`) if the token is malformed or encrypted with a different secret.

with-page-auth-required.title: withPageAuthRequired
with-page-auth-required.description:
Protects a page from unauthenticated access. Redirects to the login page and returns the
user to the original URL after a successful login.

**App Router**: pass an async page function. The SDK invokes it only when a session exists.
Because Server Components do not know their own URL, pass `returnTo` explicitly if you need
the user to land back on the same page after login.

**Pages Router**: call with no arguments (or an options object) to protect `getServerSideProps`.

parameters.name: fn
parameters.description: App Router: the async page component to wrap.

with-api-auth-required.title: withApiAuthRequired
with-api-auth-required.description:
Protects an API route handler. Unauthenticated requests are automatically rejected with
`401 Unauthorized`. Works with both App Router Route Handlers and Pages Router API routes.

start-interactive-login.title: startInteractiveLogin
start-interactive-login.description:
Initiates a login redirect from a Server Action or Route Handler.
Use when you need to programmatically trigger the login flow rather than
relying on the automatic redirect from `withPageAuthRequired`.

returns.description: A `NextResponse` that redirects the user to the Auth0 login page.

get-token-by-backchannel-auth.title: getTokenByBackchannelAuth
get-token-by-backchannel-auth.description:
Authenticates a user via Client-Initiated Backchannel Authentication (CIBA) and returns
the token set once the user approves the request on their device.

Initiates the backchannel flow and polls Auth0 until the user approves or the request times out.
Does not require the user to be present in the browser session.

**Prerequisite:** CIBA must be enabled in the Auth0 dashboard for your application.

returns.description: The token set, and optionally ID token claims and authorization details.

throws.description: if CIBA is not enabled for your application.
if Auth0 rejects the request.

connect-account.title: connectAccount
connect-account.description:
Starts the Connect Account flow, redirecting the user to Auth0 to authorize linking a
third-party social account (e.g. GitHub, Google) to their existing profile.

**Prerequisite:** "Offline Access" must be enabled in Connection Permissions for the target
connection. The user must have an active session.

returns.description: A `NextResponse` that redirects the user to Auth0 to authorize the connection.

throws.description: if the user is not logged in.
if starting the connect flow fails. Check `err.cause` for the underlying `MyAccountApiError`.
if completing the connect flow fails. Check `err.cause` for the underlying `MyAccountApiError`.

create-fetcher.title: createFetcher
create-fetcher.description:
Creates an authenticated HTTP client that automatically injects the user's access token
as a Bearer header on every request and handles token refresh transparently.
create-fetcher.signature: createFetcher(req: NextRequest | Request | PagesRouterRequest | undefined, options: { ... }): Promise<Fetcher<TOutput>>

parameters.name: req
parameters.description: Request for session context. Pass `undefined` in App Router; required in Pages Router.

returns.description: A `Fetcher` instance. Call `fetcher.fetchWithAuth(path, init?)` to make authenticated requests.

throws.description: (`"missing_session"`) if the user is not authenticated.

abstract-session-store.title: AbstractSessionStore
abstract-session-store.description:
Base class for custom session storage. Extend this when you need to persist sessions
to a database, Redis, or any external store instead of the default encrypted cookie.

Implement three abstract methods: `get`, `set`, and `delete`. Pass your subclass to
`Auth0Client` via the `sessionStore` option.

auth0client-options.title: Auth0ClientOptions
auth0client-options.description: Configuration options for `Auth0Client`. All options can also be set via environment variables.

properties.name: allowInsecureRequests
properties.description:
Allow insecure requests to be made to the authorization server. This can be useful when testing
with a mock OIDC provider that does not support TLS, locally.
This option can only be used when NODE_ENV is not set to `production`.

properties.name: appBaseUrl
properties.description:
The URL of your application (e.g.: `http://localhost:3000`).

Can be a single URL string, or an array of allowed base URLs. When an array is
provided, the SDK validates the incoming request origin against the list and uses
the matching entry (allow-list mode). This is useful for multi-domain or preview
deployments where you want to restrict which origins are accepted.

If it's not specified, it will be loaded from the `APP_BASE_URL` environment variable.
Multiple origins can be provided as a comma-separated string (e.g. `https://app.example.com,https://myapp.vercel.app`).
If neither is provided, the SDK will infer it from the request host at runtime.

properties.name: authorizationParameters
properties.description: Additional parameters to send to the `/authorize` endpoint.

properties.name: beforeSessionSaved
properties.description:
A method to manipulate the session before persisting it.

See [beforeSessionSaved](https://github.com/auth0/nextjs-auth0/blob/main/EXAMPLES.md#beforesessionsaved) for additional details

properties.name: clientAssertionSigningAlg
properties.description:
The algorithm used to sign the client assertion JWT.
Uses one of `token_endpoint_auth_signing_alg_values_supported` if not specified.
If the Authorization Server discovery document does not list `token_endpoint_auth_signing_alg_values_supported`
this property will be required.

properties.name: clientAssertionSigningKey
properties.description:
Private key for use with `private_key_jwt` clients.
This should be a string that is the contents of a PEM file or a CryptoKey.

properties.name: clientId
properties.description:
The Auth0 client ID.

If it's not specified, it will be loaded from the `AUTH0_CLIENT_ID` environment variable.

properties.name: clientSecret
properties.description:
The Auth0 client secret.

If it's not specified, it will be loaded from the `AUTH0_CLIENT_SECRET` environment variable.

properties.name: discoveryCache
properties.description:
Configuration for the OIDC discovery metadata cache.
Controls TTL and maximum cached issuers for MCD resolver mode.
Also applies in static mode (single cached entry).

properties.name: domain
properties.description:
The Auth0 domain for the tenant.

- `string`: Static domain (e.g., `"example.us.auth0.com"`). Existing behavior preserved.
- `DomainResolver`: Async function resolving domain per-request from headers.
  Enables Multiple Custom Domains (MCD) for B2C multi-brand, B2B SaaS, or domain migration.

Falls back to `AUTH0_DOMAIN` environment variable if not provided.
see:
- DomainResolver for resolver signature and examples.
 - [MCD Examples](https://github.com/auth0/nextjs-auth0/blob/main/EXAMPLES.md#multiple-custom-domains-mcd)

properties.name: dpopKeyPair
properties.description:
ES256 key pair for DPoP proof generation.

If not provided when `useDPoP` is true, the SDK will attempt to load keys from
environment variables `AUTH0_DPOP_PUBLIC_KEY` and `AUTH0_DPOP_PRIVATE_KEY`.
Keys must be in PEM format and use the P-256 elliptic curve.
see:
- DpopKeyPair for the key pair interface
 - generateDpopKeyPair for generating new key pairs

properties.name: dpopOptions
properties.description:
Configuration options for DPoP timing validation and retry behavior.

These options control how the SDK validates DPoP proof timing and handles
nonce errors. Proper configuration is important for both security and reliability.

properties.name: enableAccessTokenEndpoint
properties.description:
Boolean value to enable the `/auth/access-token` endpoint for use in the client app.

Defaults to `true`.

NOTE: Set this to `false` if your client does not need to directly interact with resource servers (Token Mediating Backend). This will be false for most apps.

A security best practice is to disable this to avoid exposing access tokens to the client app.

See: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-browser-based-apps#name-token-mediating-backend

properties.name: enableConnectAccountEndpoint
properties.description: If true, the `/auth/connect` endpoint will be mounted to enable users to connect additional accounts.

properties.name: enableTelemetry
properties.description:
Boolean value to opt-out of sending the library name and version to your authorization server
via the `Auth0-Client` header. Defaults to `true`.

properties.name: httpTimeout
properties.description:
Integer value for the HTTP timeout in milliseconds for authentication requests.
Defaults to `5000` ms.

properties.name: includeIdTokenHintInOIDCLogoutUrl
properties.description:
Configure whether to include id_token_hint in OIDC logout URLs.

**Recommended (default)**: Set to `true` to include `id_token_hint` parameter.
Auth0 recommends using `id_token_hint` for secure logout as per the
OIDC specification.

**Alternative approach**: Set to `false` if your application cannot securely
store ID tokens. When disabled, only `logout_hint` (session ID), `client_id`,
and `post_logout_redirect_uri` are sent.

properties.name: logoutStrategy
properties.description:
Configure the logout strategy to use.

- `'auto'` (default): Attempts OIDC RP-Initiated Logout first, falls back to `/v2/logout` if not supported
- `'oidc'`: Always uses OIDC RP-Initiated Logout (requires RP-Initiated Logout to be enabled)
- `'v2'`: Always uses the Auth0 `/v2/logout` endpoint (supports wildcards in allowed logout URLs)

properties.name: mfaTokenTtl
properties.description:
MFA context TTL in seconds. Controls how long encrypted mfa_token remains valid.
Default: 300 (5 minutes, matching Auth0's mfa_token expiration)

Can also be set via AUTH0_MFA_TOKEN_TTL environment variable.

properties.name: noContentProfileResponseWhenUnauthenticated
properties.description:
If true, the profile endpoint will return a 204 No Content response when the user is not authenticated
instead of returning a 401 Unauthorized response.

Defaults to `false`.

properties.name: onCallback
properties.description:
A method to handle errors or manage redirects after attempting to authenticate.

See [onCallback](https://github.com/auth0/nextjs-auth0/blob/main/EXAMPLES.md#oncallback) for additional details

properties.name: pushedAuthorizationRequests
properties.description: If enabled, the SDK will use the Pushed Authorization Requests (PAR) protocol when communicating with the authorization server.

properties.name: routes
properties.description:
Configure the paths for the authentication routes.

See [Custom routes](https://github.com/auth0/nextjs-auth0/blob/main/EXAMPLES.md#custom-routes) for additional details.

properties.name: secret
properties.description:
A 32-byte, hex-encoded secret used for encrypting cookies.

If it's not specified, it will be loaded from the `AUTH0_SECRET` environment variable.

properties.name: session
properties.description:
Configure the session timeouts and whether to use rolling sessions or not.

See [Session configuration](https://github.com/auth0/nextjs-auth0/blob/main/EXAMPLES.md#session-configuration) for additional details.

properties.name: sessionStore
properties.description:
A custom session store implementation used to persist sessions to a data store.

See [Database sessions](https://github.com/auth0/nextjs-auth0/blob/main/EXAMPLES.md#database-sessions) for additional details.

properties.name: signInReturnToPath
properties.description: The path to redirect the user to after successfully authenticating. Defaults to `/`.

properties.name: tokenRefreshBuffer
properties.description:
Number of seconds to refresh access tokens early when calling `getAccessToken`.
This is a server-side buffer applied to token expiration checks. For example,
with a buffer of 60 seconds, tokens expiring within the next minute will be
refreshed proactively when a refresh token is available.

Defaults to `0` (no early refresh).

properties.name: transactionCookie
properties.description: Configure the transaction cookie used to store the state of the authentication transaction.

properties.name: useDPoP
properties.description:
Enable DPoP (Demonstrating Proof-of-Possession) for enhanced OAuth 2.0 security.

When enabled, the SDK will:
- Generate DPoP proofs for token requests and protected resource requests
- Bind access tokens cryptographically to the client's key pair
- Prevent token theft and replay attacks
- Handle DPoP nonce errors with automatic retry logic

DPoP requires an ES256 key pair that can be provided via `dpopKeyPair` option
or loaded from environment variables `AUTH0_DPOP_PUBLIC_KEY` and `AUTH0_DPOP_PRIVATE_KEY`.
see: RFC 9449: OAuth 2.0 Demonstrating Proof-of-Possession at the Application Layer (DPoP)

pages-router-request.title: PagesRouterRequest
pages-router-request.description:
The incoming request type accepted by `getSession()`, `getAccessToken()`, and similar methods
in the Pages Router. Pass `req` from `getServerSideProps` or an API route handler.

pages-router-response.title: PagesRouterResponse
pages-router-response.description:
The response type accepted by `getSession()`, `getAccessToken()`, and `updateSession()` methods
in the Pages Router. Pass `res` from `getServerSideProps` or an API route handler.

before-session-saved-hook.title: BeforeSessionSavedHook
before-session-saved-hook.description:
Hook called before the session is saved after a successful login. Use this to modify
the session data, strip unwanted claims, or add custom fields.

on-callback-hook.title: OnCallbackHook
on-callback-hook.description:
Hook called after the authorization callback completes. Use this to handle errors,
customize the redirect, or perform post-login logic.

on-callback-context.title: OnCallbackContext
on-callback-context.description: Context object passed to the `onCallback` hook.

properties.name: appBaseUrl
properties.description: The resolved base URL for the current request, used to build safe redirects.

properties.name: connectedAccount
properties.description: The connected account information when the responseType is RESPONSE_TYPES.CONNECT_CODE

properties.name: responseType
properties.description:
The type of response expected from the authorization server.
One of RESPONSE_TYPES

properties.name: returnTo
properties.description: The URL or path the user should be redirected to after completing the transaction.

routes-options.title: RoutesOptions
routes-options.description:
Subset of route paths that can be customized via `Auth0Client` options.
Pass this to the `routes` option to override the default paths.

routes.title: Routes
routes.description: The full set of route paths used by the SDK's built-in handler.

session-cookie-options.title: SessionCookieOptions
session-cookie-options.description: Cookie configuration for the session cookie. Pass to `Auth0Client` via the `session.cookie` option.

properties.name: domain
properties.description:
Specifies the value for the Set-Cookie attribute. By default, no
domain is set, and most clients will consider the cookie to apply to only
the current domain.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The name of the session cookie.

Default: `__session`.

properties.name: path
properties.description: The path attribute of the session cookie. Will be set to '/' by default.

properties.name: sameSite
properties.description:
The sameSite attribute of the session cookie.

Default: `lax`.

properties.name: secure
properties.description:
The secure attribute of the session cookie.

Default: depends on the protocol of the application's base URL. If the protocol is `https`, then `true`, otherwise `false`.

properties.name: transient
properties.description: The transient attribute of the session cookie. When true, the cookie will not persist beyond the current session.

session-configuration.title: SessionConfiguration
session-configuration.description: Session lifetime configuration. Pass to `Auth0Client` via the `session` option.

properties.name: absoluteDuration
properties.description:
The absolute duration after which the session will expire. The value must be specified in seconds.

Once the absolute duration has been reached, the session will no longer be extended.

Default: 3 days.

properties.name: cookie
properties.description: The options for the session cookie.

properties.name: inactivityDuration
properties.description:
The duration of inactivity after which the session will expire. The value must be specified in seconds.

The session will be extended as long as it was active before the inactivity duration has been reached.

Default: 1 day.

properties.name: rolling
properties.description:
A boolean indicating whether rolling sessions should be used or not.

When enabled, the session will continue to be extended as long as it is used within the inactivity duration.
Once the upper bound, set via the `absoluteDuration`, has been reached, the session will no longer be extended.

Default: `true`.

session-store-options.title: SessionStoreOptions
session-store-options.description:
Options passed to `AbstractSessionStore` subclass constructor.
Extends `SessionConfiguration` with the required `secret` and optional custom `store`.

properties.name: absoluteDuration
properties.description:
The absolute duration after which the session will expire. The value must be specified in seconds.

Once the absolute duration has been reached, the session will no longer be extended.

Default: 3 days.

properties.name: cookie
properties.description: The options for the session cookie.

properties.name: inactivityDuration
properties.description:
The duration of inactivity after which the session will expire. The value must be specified in seconds.

The session will be extended as long as it was active before the inactivity duration has been reached.

Default: 1 day.

properties.name: rolling
properties.description:
A boolean indicating whether rolling sessions should be used or not.

When enabled, the session will continue to be extended as long as it is used within the inactivity duration.
Once the upper bound, set via the `absoluteDuration`, has been reached, the session will no longer be extended.

Default: `true`.

session-data.title: SessionData
session-data.description:
The full session object stored by the SDK. Available in `beforeSessionSaved` and
`onCallback` hooks, and returned by `getSession()`.

session-data-store.title: SessionDataStore
session-data-store.description:
Interface for a custom session data store. Implement this and pass it to
`AbstractSessionStore` to use a custom backend (Redis, database, etc.) for sessions.

**TTL contract:** every successful write method (`set`, `update`) must reset the session
TTL/expiry so that active sessions are not silently expired between requests.

user.title: User
user.description:
The authenticated user object available on `session.user`.
Standard OIDC claims plus any custom claims added via Auth0 Actions.

properties.name: org_id
properties.description:
The organization ID that the user belongs to.
This field is populated when the user logs in through an organization.

logout-token.title: LogoutToken
logout-token.description: The logout token shape passed to `SessionDataStore.deleteByLogoutToken()`.

logout-strategy.title: LogoutStrategy
logout-strategy.description:
Controls which logout endpoint the SDK uses.

- `"auto"` (default): uses OIDC RP-Initiated Logout if the discovery document advertises it, otherwise falls back to the Auth0 `/v2/logout` endpoint.
- `"oidc"`: always use OIDC RP-Initiated Logout.
- `"v2"`: always use the Auth0 `/v2/logout` endpoint.

properties.name: authorizationParameters
properties.description: Authorization parameters to be passed to the authorization server.

properties.name: returnTo
properties.description: The URL to redirect to after a successful login.

authorization-parameters.title: AuthorizationParameters
authorization-parameters.description:
Authorization parameters passed to the Auth0 `/authorize` endpoint.
Use these to control the login experience: scopes, audience, organization, and more.

properties.name: audience
properties.description: The unique identifier of the target API you want to access.

properties.name: max_age
properties.description: The maximum amount of time, in seconds, after which a user must reauthenticate.

properties.name: organization
properties.description:
The unique identifier of the organization that the user should be logged into.
When specified, the user will be prompted to log in to this specific organization.
The organization ID will be included in the user's session after successful authentication.

properties.name: redirect_uri
properties.description: The URL to which the authorization server will redirect the user after granting authorization.

properties.name: scope
properties.description:
The scope of the access request, expressed as a list of space-delimited, case-sensitive strings.
Defaults to `"openid profile email offline_access"`.

properties.name: audience
properties.description:
Please note: If you are passing audience, ensure that the used audiences and scopes are
part of the Application's Refresh Token Policies in Auth0 when configuring Multi-Resource Refresh Tokens (MRRT).
Auth0 Documentation on Multi-resource Refresh Tokens

properties.name: connection
properties.description: The connection name for which you want to retrieve the access token.

properties.name: login_hint
properties.description: An optional login hint to pass to the authorization server.

properties.name: subject_token_type
properties.description:
The type of token that is being exchanged.

Uses the SUBJECT_TOKEN_TYPES enum with the following allowed values:
- `SUBJECT_TYPE_REFRESH_TOKEN`: `"urn:ietf:params:oauth:token-type:refresh_token"`
- `SUBJECT_TYPE_ACCESS_TOKEN`: `"urn:ietf:params:oauth:token-type:access_token"`

Defaults to `SUBJECT_TYPE_REFRESH_TOKEN`.

connection-token-set.title: ConnectionTokenSet
connection-token-set.description: A connection access token set returned by `getAccessTokenForConnection()`.

properties.name: actorToken
properties.description:
Actor token for delegation/impersonation scenarios (RFC 8693).
If provided, `actorTokenType` is required.

properties.name: actorTokenType
properties.description: Actor token type URI (required if actorToken is provided).

properties.name: additionalParameters
properties.description:
Additional custom parameters passed to the token endpoint.
Accessible in Auth0 Action via `event.request.body`.

properties.name: audience
properties.description: The unique identifier of the target API.

properties.name: organization
properties.description: Organization ID or name for multi-tenant scenarios.

properties.name: scope
properties.description:
Space-delimited OAuth 2.0 scopes.

**Note**: These scopes are merged with SDK default scopes
(openid profile email offline_access). Duplicates are removed.

properties.name: subjectToken
properties.description:
The external token being exchanged.
This will be validated by your Auth0 Action with the Custom Token Exchange trigger.

**Validation**: Must be a non-empty string.

properties.name: subjectTokenType
properties.description:
Custom URI identifying the token type.

**Validation Rules**:
- Must be 10-100 characters
- Must be a valid URI (URL or URN format)

properties.name: accessToken
properties.description: The access token issued by Auth0

properties.name: expiresIn
properties.description: Token lifetime in seconds

properties.name: idToken
properties.description: The ID token, if openid scope was requested

properties.name: refreshToken
properties.description: The refresh token, if offline_access scope was requested

properties.name: scope
properties.description: Granted scopes

properties.name: tokenType
properties.description: Token type, typically "Bearer" or "DPoP"

backchannel-authentication-options.title: BackchannelAuthenticationOptions
backchannel-authentication-options.description:
Options for initiating a Client-Initiated Backchannel Authentication (CIBA) flow
via `getTokenByBackchannelAuth()`.

properties.name: authorizationDetails
properties.description: Optional authorization details for Rich Authorization Requests (RAR).

properties.name: authorizationParams
properties.description: Authorization parameters to be sent with the authorization request.

properties.name: bindingMessage
properties.description: Human-readable message displayed at both the consumption device and the authentication device.

properties.name: loginHint
properties.description: The login hint identifying which user to authenticate.

properties.name: requestedExpiry
properties.description: Custom expiry time for the CIBA flow in seconds. Defaults to 300 seconds (5 minutes).

backchannel-authentication-response.title: BackchannelAuthenticationResponse
backchannel-authentication-response.description: Response from a successful Client-Initiated Backchannel Authentication (CIBA) flow.

properties.name: authorizationParams
properties.description: Authorization parameters to be passed to the authorization server.

properties.name: connection
properties.description: The name of the connection to link the account with (e.g., 'google-oauth2', 'facebook').

properties.name: returnTo
properties.description: The URL to redirect to after successfully connecting the account.

properties.name: scopes
properties.description: Array of scopes to request from the Identity Provider during the connect account flow.

mfa-client.title: MfaClient
mfa-client.description:
The `auth0.mfa` client interface. Access via `auth0.mfa` on an `Auth0Client` instance.
Use this to drive MFA challenge-response and enrollment flows after receiving a `MfaRequiredError`.

authenticator.title: Authenticator
authenticator.description: An enrolled MFA authenticator returned by `auth0.mfa.getAuthenticators()`.

properties.name: active
properties.description: Whether this authenticator is active

properties.name: authenticatorType
properties.description: Authenticator type (e.g., 'otp', 'oob')

properties.name: id
properties.description: Authenticator ID

properties.name: bindingMethod
properties.description: Binding method (for oob challenges, e.g., 'prompt')

properties.name: challengeType
properties.description: Challenge type (otp, oob)

properties.name: oobCode
properties.description: OOB code (for oob challenges)

enroll-options.title: EnrollOptions
enroll-options.description:
MFA enrollment options passed to `auth0.mfa.enroll()`. Discriminated union of
`EnrollOtpOptions` and `EnrollOobOptions`.

enroll-otp-options.title: EnrollOtpOptions
enroll-otp-options.description: Options for enrolling an OTP (TOTP) authenticator via `auth0.mfa.enroll()`.

properties.name: authenticatorTypes
properties.description: Authenticator types to enroll

properties.name: mfaToken
properties.description: Encrypted MFA token

enroll-oob-options.title: EnrollOobOptions
enroll-oob-options.description: Options for enrolling an OOB (SMS/Voice/Push/Email) authenticator via `auth0.mfa.enroll()`.

properties.name: authenticatorTypes
properties.description: Authenticator types to enroll

properties.name: email
properties.description: Email address (optional for email channel)

properties.name: mfaToken
properties.description: Encrypted MFA token

properties.name: oobChannels
properties.description: OOB channels (sms, voice, auth0, email)

properties.name: phoneNumber
properties.description: Phone number in E.164 format (required for sms/voice)

enrollment-response.title: EnrollmentResponse
enrollment-response.description:
Response from `auth0.mfa.enroll()`. Discriminated union by `authenticatorType`.
Contains enrollment details including TOTP secret/barcode URI (OTP) or OOB code (SMS/Push).

verify-mfa-options.title: VerifyMfaOptions
verify-mfa-options.description:
Options for `auth0.mfa.verify()`. Discriminated union: pass `otp`, `oobCode + bindingCode`,
or `recoveryCode` depending on the challenge type.

verify-mfa-options-base.title: VerifyMfaOptionsBase
verify-mfa-options-base.description: Base options for `auth0.mfa.verify()`. Extended by each verification method.

properties.name: mfaToken
properties.description: Encrypted MFA token

verify-mfa-with-otp-options.title: VerifyMfaWithOtpOptions
verify-mfa-with-otp-options.description: Verification with OTP code from an authenticator app (TOTP).

properties.name: mfaToken
properties.description: Encrypted MFA token

properties.name: otp
properties.description: 6-digit OTP code from authenticator app

verify-mfa-with-oob-options.title: VerifyMfaWithOobOptions
verify-mfa-with-oob-options.description: Verification with OOB code sent via SMS/Email/Push.

properties.name: bindingCode
properties.description: Binding code received via SMS/Push

properties.name: mfaToken
properties.description: Encrypted MFA token

properties.name: oobCode
properties.description: OOB code from `challenge()` response

properties.name: mfaToken
properties.description: Encrypted MFA token

properties.name: recoveryCode
properties.description: Recovery code from enrollment

mfa-verify-response.title: MfaVerifyResponse
mfa-verify-response.description: Raw token response from `auth0.mfa.verify()`. Uses snake_case to match Auth0 API conventions.

properties.name: access_token
properties.description: Access token

properties.name: audience
properties.description: API audience

properties.name: expires_in
properties.description: Expires in seconds

properties.name: id_token
properties.description: ID token (if present)

properties.name: recovery_code
properties.description: Recovery code (if regenerated by tenant config)

properties.name: refresh_token
properties.description: Refresh token (if present)

properties.name: scope
properties.description: Token scope

properties.name: token_type
properties.description: Token type (usually "Bearer")

dpop-key-pair.title: DpopKeyPair
dpop-key-pair.description:
ES256 key pair for DPoP (Demonstrating Proof-of-Possession).
Generate with `generateDpopKeyPair()` and pass to `Auth0Client` via `dpopKeyPair`.

properties.name: privateKey
properties.description: ES256 private key used to sign DPoP proofs

properties.name: publicKey
properties.description: ES256 public key included in DPoP proofs for verification

dpop-options.title: DpopOptions
dpop-options.description: DPoP timing validation options. Pass to `Auth0Client` via `dpopOptions`.

properties.name: clockSkew
properties.description:
Clock skew adjustment in seconds. Positive values shift the assumed current time forward;
negative values shift it backward. Default: `0`.

properties.name: clockTolerance
properties.description:
Clock tolerance in seconds for DPoP proof DateTime claims validation.
Higher values are more permissive but may weaken replay protection. Default: `30`.

properties.name: retry
properties.description: Configuration for DPoP nonce error retry behavior.

retry-config.title: RetryConfig
retry-config.description: Retry configuration for DPoP nonce errors. Pass to `DpopOptions.retry`.

properties.name: delay
properties.description: Delay in milliseconds before retrying on DPoP nonce error. Default: `100`.

properties.name: jitter
properties.description:
Whether to add jitter (randomness) to the retry delay to prevent thundering herd.
When enabled, actual delay is 50-100% of `delay`. Default: `true`.

subject-token-types.title: SUBJECT_TOKEN_TYPES
subject-token-types.description: Subject token type URIs for Custom Token Exchange (RFC 8693).

members.name: SUBJECT_TYPE_ACCESS_TOKEN
members.description: Indicates that the token is an OAuth 2.0 access token issued by the given authorization server.

members.name: SUBJECT_TYPE_REFRESH_TOKEN
members.description: Indicates that the token is an OAuth 2.0 refresh token issued by the given authorization server.

grant-type-custom-token-exchange.title: GRANT_TYPE_CUSTOM_TOKEN_EXCHANGE
grant-type-custom-token-exchange.description: Grant type for Custom Token Exchange as per RFC 8693.

default-id-token-claims.title: DEFAULT_ID_TOKEN_CLAIMS
default-id-token-claims.description: The default set of OIDC claims stored in the session user object.

filter-default-id-token-claims.title: filterDefaultIdTokenClaims
filter-default-id-token-claims.description:
Filters a claims object to keep only the standard OIDC claims listed in `DEFAULT_ID_TOKEN_CLAIMS`.
All other claims are stripped.

Useful inside a `beforeSessionSaved` hook when the ID token includes custom claims you do not
want to store in the session.

parameters.name: claims
parameters.description: The raw claims object, typically `session.user` from the `beforeSessionSaved` callback.

returns.description: A `User` object containing only the default claims.

generate-dpop-key-pair.title: generateDpopKeyPair
generate-dpop-key-pair.description:
Generates an ES256 key pair for DPoP (Demonstrating Proof-of-Possession).
Pass the result to `Auth0Client` via the `dpopKeyPair` option to bind access tokens
cryptographically to the client, preventing token theft and replay attacks.

Call this once when your application starts and reuse the same key pair for the lifetime
of the `Auth0Client` instance.

returns.description: A `DpopKeyPair` containing ES256 `publicKey` and `privateKey` as `CryptoKey` objects.

auth0provider.title: Auth0Provider
auth0provider.description:
Bridges your server-side session to client components. Wrap your root layout with this
provider and pass the session user; useUser will return that user immediately
on first render with no loading state and no network request.

Without this provider, useUser still works but always starts as `isLoading: true`
while it fetches `/auth/profile`, causing a flash of unauthenticated state on every page load.
SWR revalidates the session in the background (on tab focus, reconnect) regardless.

**Prerequisite:** Call `auth0.getSession()` in your server layout and pass `session?.user` here.

use-user.title: useUser
use-user.description:
Returns the logged-in user's profile inside a React component.

**Prerequisite:** Auth0Provider must wrap the component tree.

returns.description:
`{ user, isLoading, error, invalidate }`. `user` is `null` when logged out
and `undefined` while loading. Call `invalidate()` to force a fresh profile fetch.

Sample `user` object:
```json
{
  "sub": "auth0|64b2e5f8a3c1d90012ef4567",
  "name": "Jane Doe",
  "nickname": "jane",
  "given_name": "Jane",
  "family_name": "Doe",
  "picture": "https://cdn.auth0.com/avatars/jd.png",
  "email": "jane@example.com",
  "email_verified": true,
  "org_id": "org_abc123"
}
```

client-with-page-auth-required.title: withPageAuthRequired
client-with-page-auth-required.description:
Protects a React page. Unauthenticated users are automatically redirected to login
and returned to the same page afterwards. The wrapped component always receives a
`user` prop containing the logged-in user's profile.

**Prerequisite:** Auth0 login route (`/auth/login`) must be configured.

generate-session-cookie.title: generateSessionCookie
generate-session-cookie.description:
Creates an encrypted session cookie that simulates a logged-in user in tests,
bypassing the real Auth0 login flow entirely.

Set the returned string as the `appSession` cookie on your test HTTP requests
to make the SDK treat the request as authenticated.

**Prerequisite:** `config.secret` must match the `AUTH0_SECRET` in your SDK
configuration, otherwise the SDK will reject the cookie as invalid.

parameters.name: session
parameters.description: Session data to encode. `user` is required; `internal` is auto-populated.

returns.description: The encrypted cookie string. Expires 1 hour from the time of generation.
